prompt: Documentary-style wide-angle photograph in a single continuous concrete environment with no seams, no divisions, and no visual splits.In the foreground, a single person stands alone, carrying two distinct structures: a large, dense, dark solid cube attached to their lower body, and a wide, complex wireframe structure extending outward from their upper body. Both are significantly large and visually heavy, dominating the frame.In the midground, slightly farther away but clearly visible, a couple stands together. Each person carries a small, simple solid cube, much smaller and lighter than the structures carried by the single individual.All subjects stand on the same continuous ground plane, under identical lighting, with no boundaries or separation between them.The scale difference is immediately clear: the single individual carries far greater total volume and complexity than either person in the couple.Mood: neutral, analytical, observational Color palette: muted greys, minimal tones Composition: depth-based comparison, no symmetry split, continuous space  Photorealistic, high detail No text, no labels, no panels, no splits, no seams, no collage, no visual divisions
insight: Single people score higher on both loneliness AND personal growth than partnered people — solitude breaks you and builds you at the same time.
